title: Elite Trader Funding Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Elite Trader Funding Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Elite Trader Funding (4.1★, $75/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Elite Trader Funding — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Elite Trader Funding?

Elite Trader Funding is a futures-exclusive prop firm that lets traders demonstrate their edge on paper accounts before accessing live capital. Founded in 2022 and headquartered in Wilmington, Delaware, the firm specializes in futures day traders and scalpers, offering six different evaluation paths to suit various experience levels and trading styles. With a 4.1/5 rating and starting costs as low as $75/month (before promotional discounts), Elite Trader Funding attracts traders seeking flexibility, immediate funding options, and a transparent profit-split structure. The platform stands out for its 100% profit split on the first $12,500 in lifetime simulated-funded profits and accounts up to $300,000, with some traders reaching live funding the same day they complete their evaluation.

How to Create Your Elite Trader Funding Account

Step 1: Visit the Elite Trader Funding Website and Choose Your Evaluation Type

Start by going to the Elite Trader Funding portal and selecting one of the six evaluation options: 1-Step Evaluation, Fast Track, Diamond Hands, Direct to Funded, or their proprietary LIVE ELITE program. Each has different requirements, holding periods, and profit targets. New traders typically start with the 1-Step or Fast Track plans, while experienced traders often jump directly to Diamond Hands or Direct to Funded to compress their timeline to live funding.

Step 2: Complete Your Account Registration

Provide your full legal name, email address, phone number, and residential address. You'll also create a username and password. The process takes about 2–3 minutes. Elite Trader Funding requires you to be at least 18 years old and a legal resident of an eligible jurisdiction (most U.S. states are supported, though some restrictions apply).

Step 3: Verify Your Identity

Submit a valid government-issued photo ID (passport, driver's license, or national ID). You may also need to provide proof of address, such as a utility bill or bank statement dated within the last 90 days. This verification typically completes within 24 hours, though sometimes it takes just a few hours during business days.

Step 4: Select Your Account Size and Pay the Evaluation Fee

Choose your desired account size (typically ranging from $25,000 to $300,000 for the simulated account). Then pay your evaluation fee. For example, the 1-Step Evaluation costs $75/month, while the Direct to Funded plan costs $599 upfront. Elite Trader Funding frequently runs 50–90% promotional discounts, making entry costs significantly lower if you catch a promotion. Payment is accepted via credit card, debit card, or bank transfer.

Step 5: Set Up Your Evaluation Account and Begin Trading

Once payment is processed and verified, you'll receive instant access to your paper trading account on Elite Trader Funding's platform or integrated broker. Your simulated account is pre-funded and ready to trade immediately. Most traders can begin evaluations within 1–2 hours of account creation. Elite Trader Funding uses real-time market data and applies real trading rules (drawdown limits, daily loss limits, profit targets) to your evaluation.

Setting Up Elite Trader Funding for the First Time

Understanding the Dashboard

When you log in, you'll see your account overview, which displays your current balance, equity, profit/loss, daily drawdown used, and maximum drawdown remaining. The dashboard also shows your overall progress toward your evaluation profit target and any alerts related to risk management triggers. Take a moment to familiarize yourself with the layout—it's similar to most trading platforms, but Elite Trader Funding's layout emphasizes risk metrics prominently because drawdown management is key to passing evaluations.

Configuring Your Trading Account Settings

Navigate to the Settings section and configure your account preferences. You can set your trading hours, currency preference, order execution speed (if options are available), and data feed refresh rates. Elite Trader Funding integrates with popular brokers, so you may also link your broker integration here if you plan to trade through a connected platform rather than the built-in terminal.

Connecting Your Broker or Data Feed

Elite Trader Funding's broker integration feature allows you to connect your preferred futures broker (NinjaTrader, Thinkorswim, Futures.io, or others depending on your plan) to sync your evaluation account. This is optional—you can also trade directly on Elite Trader Funding's platform if you prefer. If you choose broker integration, authenticate with your broker credentials and authorize the connection. Syncing typically takes 5–10 minutes.

Setting Your Risk Management Parameters

Review your account's risk rules under the Risk Management tab. Understand your daily loss limit (usually 4–5% of your account), maximum drawdown limit (typically 10–15%), and minimum trading days (varies by plan). Elite Trader Funding displays these limits clearly so you can track your proximity to breaches. Some plans, like Fast Track and Direct to Funded, allow news trading with no minimum trading days, so verify your specific rules here.

Enabling Mobile App Access (Optional)

Elite Trader Funding offers a mobile app for iOS and Android. Download the app and log in with your account credentials to monitor your account on the go. The mobile app includes real-time P&L updates, trade alerts, and the ability to view your performance analytics from anywhere. This is especially useful for day traders who want to stay updated outside of their main trading hours.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Paper Trading (Simulated Evaluation Accounts)

Your entire evaluation journey uses paper trading on a simulated account. Elite Trader Funding pre-funds your account with play money and applies real risk rules (drawdown limits, daily loss caps, profit targets). This lets you prove your strategy works before risking real capital. Paper trading is available on all six evaluation types and persists for as long as you hold an active evaluation account.

2. Mobile App

The Elite Trader Funding mobile app gives you real-time access to your account balance, equity, open trades, and performance metrics from your phone. You can also place and manage trades directly from the app if your broker integration supports mobile order execution. For futures day traders and scalpers, quick mobile access can be critical during fast-moving markets, especially if you're away from your main trading terminal.

3. Broker Integration

Connect your existing broker (like NinjaTrader, Thinkorswim, or Futures.io) to Elite Trader Funding's evaluation account. This integration syncs your trades, account balance, and performance data in real-time, so you can trade using your preferred platform while your results feed back to Elite Trader Funding's tracking system. This is particularly valuable if you already have a broker setup and customized charts—you don't need to rebuild your entire trading environment.

4. Performance Analytics

Elite Trader Funding's analytics dashboard shows your win rate, average win/loss, profit factor, Sharpe ratio, and other statistical metrics broken down by day, week, or month. This data is essential for understanding your edge, identifying your most profitable trading sessions, and catching patterns in your performance. You can export your data for further analysis in a spreadsheet or dedicated trading journal.

5. Risk Management Tools

Elite Trader Funding enforces strict risk limits automatically: daily loss limits (a percentage of your account), maximum drawdown thresholds (typically 10–15%), and minimum trading days on some plans. The platform alerts you in real-time as you approach these limits, and your account halts trading if you breach them. This automated enforcement ensures you can't accidentally violate your evaluation rules.

6. Same-Day Funding Options

If you complete your evaluation on the Fast Track or Direct to Funded plans, you may receive live capital within hours of passing—not days or weeks. Once approved for live funding, Elite Trader Funding charges an $80/month activation fee, but your profits are yours to keep (split according to your tier). This rapid path appeals to experienced traders who are confident in their edge and want immediate capital access.

7. News Trading Permission

Unlike some prop firms that restrict news trading, Elite Trader Funding allows it on select plans without minimum trading day requirements. This is a major advantage for traders who specialize in news-driven market moves, FOMC announcements, or economic data releases. Check your specific plan's rules, as permissions vary.

Elite Trader Funding Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

1-Step Evaluation — $75/mo

The entry-level option designed for new to intermediate futures traders. You have one shot to reach a profit target (usually 10–12% of your account size) without breaching your drawdown or daily loss limits. If you pass, you move to a $75/mo funded account. If you fail, you can purchase another evaluation. With frequent 50–90% promotional discounts, this plan often drops to $7.50–$37.50 per month, making it the most affordable entry point. Best for traders testing a new strategy or building a track record.

Fast Track — $75/mo

Similar cost to 1-Step, but Fast Track offers looser trading rules (wider drawdown allowances, no minimum trading days, news trading allowed) and same-day or next-day funding upon passing. You typically get one or two attempts. Fast Track is ideal for experienced traders who want to compress their timeline to live capital. The $75 base cost often drops to $20–$40 with promotions.

Diamond Hands — $365/mo

A mid-tier plan offering higher account sizes and multiple evaluation attempts (usually 2–3 chances to pass within a set timeframe). Diamond Hands removes minimum trading day requirements and allows news trading. You'll reach a higher profit target than 1-Step but have more flexibility and buffer. Best for intermediate to advanced traders who want breathing room and can afford a slightly higher entry cost. Promotions typically reduce this to $150–$250/mo.

Direct to Funded — $599

The premium fast-track option: pay once and often get immediate or same-day funding if your broker integration is set up. No evaluation phase—you're trading live capital right away. Direct to Funded is only for experienced traders with proven track records. Expect to pay an additional $80/month activation fee once live, plus ongoing commissions. With 50–90% promotional discounts, this can drop to $300–$400 upfront, which is still pricey but much cheaper than bootstrapping $100K+ yourself.

Recommendation Summary:

  • Beginner traders: Start with 1-Step Evaluation during a promotional period (e.g., $10–$20 one-time cost) to test your strategy risk-free.
  • Intermediate traders: Choose Fast Track or Diamond Hands if you're confident in your edge and want faster funding access without paying the full Direct to Funded premium.
  • Advanced traders: Use Direct to Funded if you've already passed evaluations elsewhere or have substantial live trading history. The faster path to capital justifies the higher cost.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Elite Trader Funding

1. Time Your Account Purchase Around Promotions

Elite Trader Funding runs 50–90% discounts regularly (often around market open, holidays, or specific trading events). A $75 plan can drop to $7.50–$37.50. Before paying full price, check their website or email for current promotions. Savings of $30–$50 per attempt add up quickly if you need multiple evaluations.

2. Use Broker Integration to Keep Your Current Setup

Don't switch brokers or rebuild your entire trading platform just to trade Elite Trader Funding evaluations. Integrate your existing broker (NinjaTrader, Thinkorswim, etc.) and trade your evaluation account directly from your current charts and hotkeys. This reduces setup friction and lets you trade immediately without relearning a new interface.

3. Max Out Your 5 Concurrent Sim-Funded Accounts Strategically

Elite Trader Funding allows up to 5 active evaluation accounts simultaneously (reduced from 20 in September 2025). Use this to test multiple strategies in parallel: one scalping approach, one swing-trade approach, one news-trading approach, etc. This diversification increases your odds of passing at least one evaluation while minimizing idle time waiting for results.

4. Track Your Daily P&L Against Your Limits in Real-Time

Watch your daily loss limit and maximum drawdown like a hawk. Elite Trader Funding's dashboard shows these clearly, but many traders get blindsided near the end of a trading day. Set a personal stop loss well below your daily limit (e.g., if your limit is -5%, stop trading after -3%) to give yourself a safety margin. Breaching a limit means starting over.

5. Leverage Performance Analytics to Identify Your Edge**

Review Elite Trader Funding's performance analytics weekly, not just after you pass or fail. Look for patterns: What time of day do you trade best? Which market conditions favor your strategy? Export your data and compare your Sharpe ratio, win rate, and profit factor across periods. This iterative analysis tightens your edge faster than raw trial-and-error.

6. Don't Assume News Trading Is Risk-Free

Fast Track and Direct to Funded allow news trading without minimum trading days, which is a huge advantage. But volatile news moves can spike your drawdown or trigger daily loss limits faster than you expect. Have a strict stop-loss plan for news trades and account for wider spreads during announcements.

7. Plan Your Path to Live Funding Before You Start

Decide upfront whether you'll pursue same-day funding (Fast Track, Direct to Funded) or a slower funded account after passing a multi-step evaluation. Same-day options cost more upfront but compress your timeline. If you're bootstrapping capital elsewhere or have other urgent funding needs, pay for speed. If you can wait weeks, save money with the cheaper plans.

Common Elite Trader Funding Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Broker Integration Fails or Trades Aren't Syncing

If your broker integration isn't syncing your trades to Elite Trader Funding, first confirm that your broker account and Elite Trader Funding account are using the same trading account ID and that your broker has authorized the connection. Log out of both platforms, clear your browser cache, and log back in. If the problem persists, contact Elite Trader Funding's support team with your broker name and account ID—they can manually troubleshoot the API connection or recommend a workaround (such as trading directly on Elite Trader Funding's platform instead).

Issue 2: Hitting Drawdown or Daily Loss Limits Unexpectedly**

Traders often underestimate how fast losses accumulate in high-volatility markets or with tight stops. If you're consistently hitting limits early, your position size is likely too large or your strategy needs tighter risk controls. Review your average trade size and drawdown per trade using Elite Trader Funding's analytics. Reduce your lot size by 20–30%, tighten your stop-losses, or increase your profit targets to improve your risk-to-reward ratio. Rerun your strategy on paper trading with smaller positions before attempting another evaluation.

Issue 3: Mobile App Won't Load or Sync in Real-Time**

Ensure you're on the latest version of the Elite Trader Funding mobile app (check your device's app store for updates). If the app still won't load, try force-quitting the app, clearing its cache, and restarting it. If you're trading live capital and the mobile app is critical, use the web platform (https://app.elitetraders.io or similar) as a backup. Notify support if outages persist—they can provide status updates and escalate technical issues.

Issue 4: Unclear LIVE ELITE Invitation Criteria**

Elite Trader Funding's LIVE ELITE program (their proprietary top-tier offering) has opaque invitation criteria. You can't simply apply; you're invited after demonstrating exceptional trading performance across multiple funded accounts. If you're interested, maintain a high Sharpe ratio, large profit factor, and consistent profitability across multiple funded accounts and contact support to inquire about eligibility. There's no formal application process, so building a track record is your only path in.

Is Elite Trader Funding Worth It? Our Verdict

Elite Trader Funding is a solid choice for futures day traders and scalpers seeking flexible evaluation paths, rapid funding options, and a transparent profit split. Its 4.1/5 rating, competitive starting price of $75/month (often discounted to $7.50–$37.50), 100% profit split on the first $12,500, and same-day funding options on premium plans set it apart from competitors like Topstep and FundedNext. The main drawbacks are the futures-only limitation, opaque LIVE ELITE criteria, and the $80/month activation fee once funded. If you're an equities or forex trader, look elsewhere. If you're a serious futures trader with a proven strategy, Elite Trader Funding's combination of low entry cost, multiple evaluation types, and rapid capital access makes it one of the stronger prop firms in 2026. Check out our full Elite Trader Funding review for deeper analysis, and compare it to other top prop firms with our Elite Trader Funding vs Topstep guide.
